---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

```{=html}
<section class="page-banner">
  <div style="max-width:1000px;margin:0 auto;">
    <h1>About HealthScope</h1>
    <p>Health facility intelligence beyond static inventories.</p>
  </div>
</section>
```

## Mission

**HealthScope** is an open-access health facility intelligence portal designed to move beyond static facility inventories toward dynamic, demand-driven health access analytics across malaria-endemic countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. Developed from data extracted directly from Ministries of Health, HealthScope transforms raw registry data into actionable intelligence.

The portal reveals not just *where* health facilities exist, but whether populations can realistically access them and what services they offer.

## The Gap We Fill

Current approaches to health facility mapping focus primarily on documenting facility locations without deeper analysis. HealthScope goes further by providing:

- **Population-demand modelling** -- reachability analysis beyond simple location pins
- **Service-level intelligence** -- mapping of what services exist where
- **General information at scale** -- population-weighted access metrics and facility distribution analysis

HealthScope addresses these gaps as an open, reproducible data platform.

## Intelligence Layers

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(280px,1fr));gap:1.25rem;margin:2rem 0;">

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid var(--map-red,#c9a227);">
    <h3 style="color:var(--map-red,#c9a227);margin-top:0;">1. Facility Registry Lake</h3>
    <p>Standardized facility data from MoH sources across SSA countries. Multi-country harmonized schema; direct MoH sourcing.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid #059669;">
    <h3 style="color:#059669;margin-top:0;">2. Demand-Side Access</h3>
    <p>Population catchment zones using road networks; travel-time isochrones showing not just location, but reachability.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid #f59e0b;">
    <h3 style="color:#f59e0b;margin-top:0;">3. Service Gap Intelligence</h3>
    <p>Which services exist where. Per-domain coverage across the registry, interactive country switching, and per-facility service profiles.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid #06b6d4;">
    <h3 style="color:#06b6d4;margin-top:0;">4. General Information</h3>
    <p>Country-level descriptive statistics &mdash; facility counts, type mix, ownership split &mdash; that give a fast picture of the registry without claiming reachability or access modelling we have not yet built.</p>
  </div>

</div>
```

## ETL Pipeline

The HealthScope ETL pipeline ingests each Ministry of Health registry through three steps:

1. **Extract** &mdash; pull the latest country CSV / API payload (Tanzania HFR portal, Kenya KMHFR, Ethiopia MFR v2, etc.) into a country-specific raw folder.
2. **Transform** &mdash; standardise columns to a shared schema, validate coordinates against the country's bounding box, mint a stable global facility UID, and map each raw service name to the canonical 7-domain hierarchy via per-country crosswalks.
3. **Load** &mdash; publish versioned per-country CSVs (`{country}_standardized.csv` for facilities, `{country}_services_standardized.csv` for services) that the portal consumes directly via a git submodule.

### Current Pipeline Status

| Country | Facilities | Source |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| Tanzania | 13,075 | MoH HFR Portal |
| Nigeria | 51,023 | NHMIS / DHIS2 |
| Kenya | 17,353 | KMHFR |
| Uganda | 8,512 | MoH DHIS2 |
| Zambia | 3,731 | MoH HRIS |
| Malawi | 1,929 | MoH CMED |
| Botswana | 1,076 | MoH MFL |
| Ethiopia | 40,035 | MoH MFR v2 |
| **Total** | **136,734** | |

## Data Sources

HealthScope ingests **only authoritative, official sources** — Ministry of Health master facility registries and openly-licensed compilations of the same upstream data. Each country's row in the pipeline status above links back to its primary registry. For the full source catalogue with license and citation details, visit the dedicated page.

[**View Data Sources &rarr;**](data-sources.html)

## Team

The portal is maintained by a team of data engineers, GIS specialists, epidemiologists, and researchers at the Malaria Atlas Project Dar es Salaam Node.

[**Visit the Team Page &rarr;**](team.html)
